structuredRootHelp:
Usage: privacy-pools [options] [command]

Privacy Pools: a compliant way to transact privately on Ethereum

Options:
  -V, --version          output the version number
  -c, --chain <name>     Target chain (mainnet, arbitrum, optimism, ...)
  -j, --json             Machine-readable JSON output on stdout. After the
                         command name, pass --json <fields> or --json=<fields>
                         to select top-level fields.
  -o, --output <format>  Output format: table (default), csv, json, yaml, wide,
                         name (choices: "table", "csv", "json", "yaml", "wide",
                         "name")
  -y, --yes              Skip confirmation prompts
  --web                  Open the primary explorer or portal link in your
                         browser when available
  --help-brief           Show condensed command help (default)
  --help-full            Show full command help with examples, safety notes, and
                         JSON fields
  -r, --rpc-url <url>    Override RPC URL
  --template <template>  Render structured output through a lightweight
                         Mustache-style template with {{path.to.value}}
                         placeholders and {{#items}}...{{/items}} list iteration
  --agent                Machine-friendly mode (alias for --json --yes --quiet)
  -q, --quiet            Suppress human-oriented stderr output
  -v, --verbose          Enable verbose/debug output (-v info, -vv debug, -vvv
                         trace)
  --no-progress          Suppress spinners/progress indicators (useful in CI)
  --no-header            Suppress header rows in CSV and wide/tabular table
                         output
  --no-banner            Disable welcome banner output
  --no-color             Disable colored output (also respects NO_COLOR env var)
  --timeout <seconds>    Network/transaction timeout in seconds (default: 30)
  --jmes <expression>    Filter JSON output with a JMESPath expression (implies
                         --json)
  --jq <expression>      Compatibility alias for --jmes (JMESPath, not jq
                         syntax)
  --profile <name>       Use a named profile (separate wallet identity and
                         config)
  -h, --help             display help for command

Commands:
  init                   Create, load, or finish setting up your Privacy Pools
                         account
  upgrade                Check for CLI updates
  config                 View and manage CLI configuration
  flow                   Deposit and privately withdraw in one guided workflow
  simulate               Preview deposit, withdraw, and ragequit without
                         submitting
  pools                  Browse available pools
  deposit                Deposit ETH or ERC-20 tokens into a pool
  accounts               View balances, approval status, and Pool Accounts
  migrate                Check migration status for legacy Pool Accounts
  withdraw               Withdraw privately from a pool
  recipients|recents     Manage remembered withdrawal recipients
  ragequit               Recover funds publicly to your deposit address
  broadcast              Broadcast a signed envelope or relayer request built
                         elsewhere
  history                View your deposit and withdrawal history
  sync                   Sync account state with the latest onchain data
  tx-status              Check async transaction submission status
  status                 Check account setup and network status
  activity               View recent deposits and withdrawals across pools
  protocol-stats         View aggregate network statistics
  pool-stats             View statistics for one pool
  stats                  Deprecated compatibility alias for protocol-stats and
                         pool-stats
  guide                  Learn key concepts, workflows, and troubleshooting
  capabilities           Describe CLI capabilities for agents
  describe               Describe a command's flags, args, and output schema
  completion             Generate or install shell tab completion
  help                   display help for command

Common workflows:
  Easy path         privacy-pools flow start 0.1 ETH --to <address>
  Manual path       privacy-pools deposit 0.1 ETH
  Watch review      privacy-pools accounts --chain mainnet --pending-only
  Withdraw          privacy-pools withdraw --all ETH --to <address>

Help topics:
  topics, quickstart, keys, workflow, flow-states, ragequit, automation, env-vars, next-actions, profiles, pool-accounts, agents, json, formatting, modes, quiet, troubleshooting, exit-codes
  privacy-pools help <topic>

  Get started:      privacy-pools init
  Full guide:       privacy-pools guide
  Command help:     privacy-pools <command> --help
  Agent discovery:  privacy-pools capabilities
  Agent skill:      skills/privacy-pools-cli/SKILL.md

helpTextByPath.init:
[1m[38;5;110mUsage:[39m[22m [1mprivacy-pools init [options][22m

Create, load, or finish setting up your Privacy Pools account

[1m[38;5;110mOptions:[39m[22m
  [38;5;179m--recovery-phrase <phrase>[39m     Import an existing recovery phrase (unsafe:
                                 visible in process list)
  [38;5;179m--recovery-phrase-file <path>[39m  Import recovery phrase from a file (raw phrase
                                 or Privacy Pools backup file)
  [38;5;179m--recovery-phrase-stdin[39m        Import recovery phrase from stdin (raw phrase
                                 or Privacy Pools backup text)
  [38;5;179m--show-recovery-phrase[39m         Include generated recovery phrase in JSON
                                 output (unsafe: may be logged or piped)
  [38;5;179m--backup-file <path>[39m           Write a generated recovery phrase backup to a
                                 file
  [38;5;179m--private-key <key>[39m            Set the signer private key (unsafe: visible in
                                 process list)
  [38;5;179m--private-key-file <path>[39m      Set the signer private key from a file
  [38;5;179m--private-key-stdin[39m            Set the signer private key from stdin
  [38;5;179m--signer-only[39m                  Add or replace the signer key without changing
                                 the recovery phrase
  [38;5;179m--default-chain <chain>[39m        Set default chain
  [38;5;179m--rpc-url <url>[39m                Set RPC URL for the default chain
  [38;5;179m--force[39m                        Overwrite existing configuration without
                                 prompting
  [38;5;179m--dry-run[39m                      Preview the init changes without writing files
                                 or generating a live recovery phrase
  [38;5;179m--staged[39m                       Emit onboarding progress as JSONL envelopes in
                                 --json/--agent mode (preflight, recovery,
                                 backup, signer, chain, write, discovery,
                                 complete)
  [38;5;179m-h, --help[39m                     display help for command

Use only one secret stdin source per run: either --recovery-phrase-stdin or --private-key-stdin.


Flag guide:
  Setup mode:
    --signer-only
    --force
    --dry-run
    --staged
  Secret sources:
    --recovery-phrase <phrase>
    --recovery-phrase-file <path>
    --recovery-phrase-stdin
    --backup-file <path>
    --show-recovery-phrase
    --private-key <key>
    --private-key-file <path>
    --private-key-stdin
  Network:
    --default-chain <chain>
    --rpc-url <url>
  Output & Defaults:
    --yes
    --agent
    --help-brief
    --help-full

Tip: add --help-full for examples, safety notes, and JSON fields.

                              workflow or submitting a deposit

                              pauses
  [38;5;179m--stream-json[39m               Emit line-delimited JSON progress events and
                              finish with the final flow envelope

  [38;5;179m--direct[39m                                    WILL publicly link deposit and withdrawal addresses onchain. This cannot be undone.
  [38;5;179m--confirm-direct-withdraw[39m                   Deprecated: replaced by interactive confirmation. Will be removed in v3.x.

  [38;5;179m--no-wait[39m                                   Return after submission instead of waiting for confirmation
  [38;5;179m--stream-json[39m                               Emit line-delimited JSON progress events and finish with the final withdrawal envelope

guideStructuredPayload.help:
Privacy Pools: Quick Guide

Available Topics
  quickstart        Install, setup, and first deposit
  keys              Two-key model (recovery phrase + signer key)
  workflow          Step-by-step workflow for deposits and withdrawals
  flow-states       Flow state machine and transitions
  ragequit          Public recovery to the deposit address
  automation        Global flags, env vars, and agent/CI integration
  env-vars          Environment variable fallbacks and overrides
  next-actions      How nextActions guide agent follow-up
  profiles          Named profiles and wallet/config separation
  pool-accounts     Pool Account states, approval, and recovery
  agents            Agent mode, discovery, and machine workflows
  json              JSON envelope, field selection, and JMESPath filtering
  formatting        Output formats, command names, and flag conventions
  modes             Confirmation, dry-run, unsigned, and agent modes
  quiet             Quiet mode and low-noise automation output
  troubleshooting   Common issues and fixes
  exit-codes        CLI exit codes by category

  Run privacy-pools guide <topic> to open one topic.
  Common shortcuts: guide flow, guide json, guide troubleshooting.

Install & Run
  npm i -g privacy-pools-cli
  npm i -g github:0xmatthewb/privacy-pools-cli  (unreleased/source builds)
  privacy-pools status
  privacy-pools upgrade --check                (check npm for a newer installed release)
  npm run dev -- status                        (from source, no global install)
  privacy-pools completion --help                  (shell autocomplete setup)

Quick Start
  privacy-pools init
  privacy-pools init --recovery-phrase-file <downloaded-file> (load an existing account from a website export)
  cat <downloaded-file> | privacy-pools init --recovery-phrase-stdin (stdin alternative for loading an account)
  privacy-pools init --signer-only (finish setup or replace the signer key without changing the account)
  privacy-pools flow start 0.1 ETH --to 0xRecipient          (easy path: deposit now, withdraw later)
  privacy-pools flow start 100 USDC --to 0xRecipient --new-wallet  (easy path with a dedicated workflow wallet)
  privacy-pools pools                                          (browse available pools)
  privacy-pools deposit 0.1 ETH
  privacy-pools accounts --chain mainnet --pending-only        (poll ASP review; keep the same --chain until it disappears)
  privacy-pools accounts --chain mainnet                       (then confirm approved vs declined vs POA Needed)
  privacy-pools withdraw 0.05 ETH --to 0xRecipient --pool-account PA-1
  Transaction commands use your default chain (set during init).
  Public dashboards like pools/activity/protocol-stats default to CLI-supported mainnet chains.
  Use --include-testnets to include supported testnets, or --chain to scope one network.
  Accounts is wallet-dependent: use --chain to keep approval checks on the same network as the deposit.

  Deposits are reviewed by the 0xBow ASP before approval.
  Most deposits are approved within 1 hour, but some may take longer (up to 7 days).
  ASP approval is required for withdraw, including --direct. Ragequit is your
  self-custody guarantee, always available to publicly recover funds to the
  original deposit address. Declined saved easy-path workflows use
  'flow ragequit' as their canonical public recovery path, and operators can
  also choose it manually after the public deposit exists.

Recovery Phrase and Signer Key
  Recovery phrase  restores this Privacy Pools account and enables private withdrawals
  Signer key       pays gas and sends transactions (can be set later with init --signer-only)
  The signer key may come from the same wallet as your recovery phrase or from a separate key you control.
  Note: PRIVACY_POOLS_PRIVATE_KEY env var takes precedence over a saved key file.
  Exception: flow start --new-wallet creates and uses a dedicated per-workflow wallet instead of the configured signer.

Workflow
  1. init           Set up your Privacy Pools account and config (run once)
  2. flow start     Easy path: deposit now and save a later private withdrawal
  3. flow watch     Human path: stay attached through funding, approval, delay, and withdrawal
  4. flow status    Agent path: poll the saved workflow snapshot without mutating it
  5. flow step      Agent path: advance at most one saved-workflow step
  6. flow ragequit  Self-custody recovery for saved workflows (always available)
  7. pools          Manual path: browse available pools
  8. deposit        Manual path: deposit into a pool (ASP vetting fee shown before confirming)
  9. accounts       Manual path: poll pending review, then confirm approval status and balances
 10. migrate status Read-only legacy check on CLI-supported chains
 11. withdraw       Manual path: withdraw privately (once approved; fee shown before confirming)
 12. history        View transaction history
  *  status         Check setup and connection health (checks run by default)
  *  upgrade        Check npm for updates or upgrade this CLI
  *  activity       Public onchain feed (for your history, use 'history')
  *  ragequit       Self-custody guarantee. Publicly recovers funds to deposit address
  *  withdraw quote Check relayer fees before withdrawing
  'migrate status' is read-only. The CLI does not submit migration transactions; use the website for actual migration or website-based recovery.
  It only checks chains currently supported by the CLI; review beta or website-only legacy migration surfaces in the website.
  Human 'flow start --new-wallet' waits for funding automatically; '--agent' returns an awaiting_funding snapshot instead.
  In machine mode, this path requires '--export-new-wallet <path>' so the generated key is backed up first.
  Manual commands remain available for advanced control.

Flow States
  awaiting_funding                    Workflow wallet needs ETH/tokens to proceed.
  depositing_publicly                 Deposit transaction submitted, awaiting onchain confirmation.
  awaiting_asp                        Deposit confirmed, ASP is reviewing.
  approved_waiting_privacy_delay      Approved; privacy delay timer running.
  approved_ready_to_withdraw          Ready for private withdrawal.
  withdrawing                         Withdrawal transaction in flight.
  completed                           Private withdrawal succeeded.
  completed_public_recovery           Ragequit recovery completed.
  paused_poa_required                 Proof of Association needed before private withdrawal.
  paused_declined                     Deposit declined; use flow ragequit for public recovery.
  stopped_external                    Workflow stopped by external event.

Ragequit / Public Recovery
  privacy-pools ragequit ETH --pool-account PA-1
  privacy-pools flow ragequit latest

  Ragequit publicly recovers a Pool Account to the original deposit address.
  It is your self-custody fallback when ASP approval is unavailable, a saved
  flow is declined, the relayer minimum blocks a saved full-balance withdrawal,
  or you explicitly choose public recovery instead of waiting.

  This does not provide privacy for that Pool Account. Prefer withdraw or
  flow watch when the account can continue through the private relayed path.
  In the website this corresponds to the Exit path.

Global Options
  -c, --chain <name>    Target chain (mainnet, arbitrum, optimism; testnets: sepolia, op-sepolia)
  -r, --rpc-url <url>   Override RPC URL
  -j, --json            Machine-readable JSON output
  --output <fmt>        Output format: table (default), csv, json, yaml, wide, name
  --no-color            Disable colored output (also respects NO_COLOR env var)
  -y, --yes             Skip confirmation prompts
  -q, --quiet           Suppress human-oriented stderr output
  -v, --verbose         Enable verbose/debug output (-v info, -vv debug, -vvv trace)
  --no-progress         Suppress spinners/progress indicators (useful in CI)
  --no-header          Suppress header rows in CSV and wide/tabular table output
  --agent               Alias for --json --yes --quiet (agent/automation mode)
  --timeout <seconds>  Network/transaction timeout (default: 30)
  --jmes <expr>        Filter JSON output with JMESPath syntax
  --jq <expr>          Compatibility alias for --jmes (not jq syntax)
  --template <template> Render structured output through a lightweight {{path.to.value}} template
  --web                 Open the primary explorer or portal link in your browser when available
  --no-banner           Disable welcome banner
  --profile <name>     Use a named profile (separate wallet identity and config)

Environment Variables
  PRIVACY_POOLS_HOME / PRIVACY_POOLS_CONFIG_DIR  Override the CLI config directory.
  XDG_CONFIG_HOME  Fallback config base. Used as $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/privacy-pools when no Privacy Pools override is set and no legacy ~/.privacy-pools directory exists.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_PRIVATE_KEY  Signer private key; takes precedence over the saved .signer file.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_CIRCUITS_DIR  Override the circuit artifact directory with a trusted pre-provisioned path.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RPC_URL / PP_RPC_URL  Override the RPC endpoint for all chains.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_ASP_HOST / PP_ASP_HOST  Override the ASP endpoint for all chains.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RELAYER_HOST / PP_RELAYER_HOST  Override the relayer endpoint for all chains.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RPC_URL_<CHAIN> / PP_RPC_URL_<CHAIN>  Override the RPC endpoint for one chain, for example ARBITRUM or SEPOLIA.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_ASP_HOST_<CHAIN> / PP_ASP_HOST_<CHAIN>  Override the ASP endpoint for one chain.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RELAYER_HOST_<CHAIN> / PP_RELAYER_HOST_<CHAIN>  Override the relayer endpoint for one chain.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_AGENT  Enable agent mode by default (equivalent to --agent).
  PRIVACY_POOLS_QUIET  Suppress human-oriented stderr output by default, matching --quiet.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_YES  Skip confirmation prompts by default, matching --yes.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_NO_PROGRESS  Suppress spinners/progress indicators by default, matching --no-progress.
  NO_COLOR  Disable colored output, matching --no-color.
  FORCE_COLOR  Force colored output when supported by the terminal renderer.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_BANNER / PRIVACY_POOLS_BANNER_ART  Choose the welcome illustration. Set to merkle for the alternate Merkle tree banner.
  PP_NO_UPDATE_CHECK  Set to 1 to disable the update-available notification.

Interaction Modes
  Human mode (default): interactive prompts + readable output.
  Agent mode: --agent for structured JSON output, no prompts, no banners.
  Equivalent to --json --yes --quiet.

Advanced Modes
  Unsigned mode builds transaction payloads without signing or submitting.
  Requires init (recovery phrase) for deposit secret generation.
  Does NOT require a signer key. The signing party provides their own.
  Output includes from plus description. from is null when the signer is unconstrained, and set when the protocol requires a specific caller.
  --unsigned           (default) Wrapped in JSON envelope: { schemaVersion, success, ... }
  --unsigned tx        Raw transaction array: [{ from, to, data, value, valueHex, chainId, description }]
             Raw format skips the envelope. Intended for direct piping to signing tools.
  --dry-run    Validate and generate proofs without submitting.

Environment Variable Fallbacks
  PRIVACY_POOLS_AGENT  Enable agent mode by default (equivalent to --agent).
  PRIVACY_POOLS_QUIET  Suppress human-oriented stderr output by default, matching --quiet.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_YES  Skip confirmation prompts by default, matching --yes.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_NO_PROGRESS  Suppress spinners/progress indicators by default, matching --no-progress.
  NO_COLOR  Disable colored output, matching --no-color.
  FORCE_COLOR  Force colored output when supported by the terminal renderer.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_BANNER / PRIVACY_POOLS_BANNER_ART  Choose the welcome illustration. Set to merkle for the alternate Merkle tree banner.
  PP_NO_UPDATE_CHECK  Set to 1 to disable the update-available notification.

TTY Behavior
  Interactive init, flow, deposit, withdraw, ragequit, upgrade, and recipient prompts require stdin/stdout TTYs.
  In non-TTY shells, provide the required flags or use --agent so the command fails closed with a machine-readable error instead of waiting for input.
  PP_FORCE_TTY=1 is test-only escape hatch behavior for preview runners and should not be used in automation.

Configuration Overrides
  PRIVACY_POOLS_HOME / PRIVACY_POOLS_CONFIG_DIR  Override the CLI config directory.
  XDG_CONFIG_HOME  Fallback config base. Used as $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/privacy-pools when no Privacy Pools override is set and no legacy ~/.privacy-pools directory exists.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_PRIVATE_KEY  Signer private key; takes precedence over the saved .signer file.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_CIRCUITS_DIR  Override the circuit artifact directory with a trusted pre-provisioned path.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RPC_URL / PP_RPC_URL  Override the RPC endpoint for all chains.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_ASP_HOST / PP_ASP_HOST  Override the ASP endpoint for all chains.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RELAYER_HOST / PP_RELAYER_HOST  Override the relayer endpoint for all chains.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RPC_URL_<CHAIN> / PP_RPC_URL_<CHAIN>  Override the RPC endpoint for one chain, for example ARBITRUM or SEPOLIA.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_ASP_HOST_<CHAIN> / PP_ASP_HOST_<CHAIN>  Override the ASP endpoint for one chain.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RELAYER_HOST_<CHAIN> / PP_RELAYER_HOST_<CHAIN>  Override the relayer endpoint for one chain.

Runtime Overrides
  PRIVACY_POOLS_CLI_DISABLE_NATIVE  Set to 1 to force the pure JS runtime path.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_CLI_BINARY  Advanced maintainer override for the launcher target native-shell binary path.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_CLI_JS_WORKER  Advanced maintainer override for the packaged JS worker entrypoint.

nextActions
  Success JSON responses may include nextActions[] when the CLI has a low-ambiguity follow-up to recommend.
  Each entry includes command, reason, when, cliCommand, args?, options?, and runnable?.

Runnable Semantics
  runnable=true  The cliCommand is complete and can be executed as-is.
  runnable=false The action is a template; fill in missing user input first.

Ordering
  The first matching action is the highest-priority recommendation.
  Private/resume paths come first, required public recovery comes before optional public recovery, and template deposit/withdraw actions come last.

Common Uses
  privacy-pools deposit --agent         Emits the canonical pending-review polling command.
  privacy-pools status --agent          Emits the canonical init/pools/accounts follow-up.
  privacy-pools tx-status <submissionId> --agent  Polls async write confirmation after --no-wait.
  privacy-pools flow status --agent     Emits saved-workflow poll, step, or ragequit guidance.

Profiles
  Profiles let one machine keep separate Privacy Pools config, wallet state, and signer setup under different names.
  Use --profile <name> on any command to select one profile for that invocation.

Commands
  privacy-pools config profile list
  privacy-pools config profile create <name>
  privacy-pools config profile use <name>
  privacy-pools config profile active

When To Use Them
  Use profiles when you need separate operator identities, separate test/main workflows, or isolated machine-runner state on one host.

Pool Accounts
  A Pool Account (e.g. PA-1) is your onchain deposit.
  Approval, withdrawal, and ragequit happen per Pool Account, not per wallet.

Statuses
  approved       Ready for private withdrawal
  pending        Still under ASP review
  poa_required   Proof of Association is required before private withdrawal
  declined       Private withdrawal unavailable; ragequit remains available
  spent          Already withdrawn
  exited         Ragequit completed

Useful Commands
  privacy-pools accounts --chain <chain>
  privacy-pools withdraw --pool-account PA-1 --to 0xRecipient
  privacy-pools ragequit --pool-account PA-1
  privacy-pools migrate status --agent --include-testnets  (legacy readiness only)

Agent Mode
  --agent is shorthand for --json --yes --quiet.
  Structured JSON stays on stdout. Human-oriented narration stays on stderr and is suppressed in agent mode.

Discovery
  privacy-pools capabilities --agent      Full runtime capability manifest
  privacy-pools describe withdraw --agent Command-specific flags, risks, and JSON fields
  privacy-pools describe envelope.nextActions --agent Schema-path contract fields
  privacy-pools guide next-actions --agent Shared nextActions contract

Recommended Flow
  1. privacy-pools status --agent
  2. privacy-pools init --agent --default-chain <chain> --backup-file <path>
  3. privacy-pools pools --agent --chain <chain>
  4. privacy-pools flow start <amount> <asset> --to <address> --agent --chain <chain>
  5. privacy-pools flow status [workflowId|latest] --agent
  6. privacy-pools flow step [workflowId|latest] --agent
  7. privacy-pools tx-status <submissionId> --agent  (when using --no-wait on deposit/withdraw/ragequit/broadcast)

JSON Contract
  Use --json for machine-readable output. Use --agent for --json --yes --quiet.
  Successful commands emit:
  { "schemaVersion": "2.0.0", "success": true, ...payload }
  Failed commands emit:
  { "schemaVersion": "2.0.0", "success": false, "errorCode": "...", "error": { ... } }

Filtering
  --json <fields>       Select top-level fields by comma-separated name.
  --json=<fields>       Inline form for compact scripts and automation.
  --jmes <expression>     Apply a JMESPath expression to the final envelope.
  --jq <expression>       Compatibility alias for --jmes (not jq syntax).
  --template <template>   Interpolate the final envelope with {{path.to.value}} placeholders and {{#items}}...{{/items}} list iteration.
  Unknown selected fields fail with INPUT_UNKNOWN_JSON_FIELD, availableFields[], and did-you-mean suggestions when available.
  Invalid JMESPath expressions fail before command output is emitted.
  --json <fields>, --jmes/--jq, and --template are mutually exclusive.

Discovery
  privacy-pools capabilities --agent      Full command and schema manifest.
  privacy-pools describe withdraw --agent Command-specific fields and examples.
  privacy-pools describe envelope.commands.status.successFields --agent Deep contract schema fragments.

Output Formats
  --output table  Human-readable default.
  --output wide   Human-readable wide tables on stdout for tabular list commands.
  --output csv    CSV for supported read-only list/report commands.
  --json          Structured envelopes on stdout.

Command Names
  Collection commands use plural root names: accounts, pools, recipients.
  Detail views use positionals instead of singular aliases:
  privacy-pools pools ETH      Pool detail
  privacy-pools accounts --pool-account PA-1  Pool Account detail/filter
  For discoverability, pools list and pools ls are accepted aliases for pools.

Short Flag Conventions
  -c chain, -r RPC URL, -j JSON, -o output format.
  -y yes/confirm, -q quiet, -v verbose.
  -p is page on paginated public reports; use --pool-account for Pool Account selection.
  Prefer long flags in scripts when a flag controls funds or privacy posture.

Modes
  --yes       Skip confirmation prompts. Use only after the command inputs are fully reviewed.
  --dry-run   Preview validation and generated outputs without signing or submitting.
  --unsigned  Build transaction payloads without signing or submitting; implies --yes.
  --agent     Machine mode: --json --yes --quiet.

Safety Notes
  --dry-run and --unsigned may use approximate validation because no transaction is submitted.
  Fund-moving commands still surface warnings and privacy-cost manifests where relevant.
  Prefer relayed withdrawals. withdraw --direct is a stronger privacy hazard than ragequit.

Quiet Mode
  --quiet suppresses human-oriented stderr output while keeping machine-readable stdout intact.
  Use it in CI or scripts when you want less narration but do not want JSON mode.

Status Special Case
  privacy-pools status --quiet prints one concise stdout summary line instead of going fully silent.
  That keeps automation-friendly logs from looking like a hung process.

Related Modes
  --json keeps structured envelopes on stdout.
  --agent is shorthand for --json --yes --quiet.

Troubleshooting
  Stale data?      Commands auto-sync; force a full re-sync with 'privacy-pools sync'.
  ASP unreachable?  Check 'privacy-pools status' (health checks run by default).
  Long proof time?  Proofs use bundled circuit artifacts. The first proof may spend a moment verifying them.
  Native fallback?  Set PRIVACY_POOLS_CLI_DISABLE_NATIVE=1 to force the JS launcher,
                   or see docs/runtime-upgrades.md for runtime troubleshooting and overrides.
  Upgrade path?     Run privacy-pools upgrade to check npm for updates or upgrade this CLI.
  Not approved?     Deposits are reviewed by the ASP.
                   Most deposits are approved within 1 hour, but some may take longer (up to 7 days).
                   Some may require Proof of Association or be
                   declined. Declined deposits can be recovered
                   publicly via ragequit.
  Custom RPC?       Pass --rpc-url on any command, or save per-chain overrides in
                   ~/.privacy-pools/config.json under "rpcOverrides": { "<chainId>": "https://..." }.

Exit Codes
  0  Success
  1  Unknown/general error
  2  Input/validation error
  3  RPC/network error
  4  Setup required or incomplete local configuration
  5  Relayer error
  6  Proof generation error
  7  Contract revert
  8  ASP error
  9  Prompt or confirmation cancelled


Using CLI with an Existing Website Account
  If you already use privacypools.com, you can access the same account from the CLI:
  1. Export your 12/24-word recovery phrase from the website.
  2. Run: privacy-pools init --recovery-phrase-file ./recovery.txt (or: cat recovery.txt | privacy-pools init --recovery-phrase-stdin)
  3. Set your signer key: export PRIVACY_POOLS_PRIVATE_KEY=0x...
  4. Run: privacy-pools accounts  (syncs onchain state automatically)
  If the website account was created before the 2024 security update, run privacy-pools migrate status to inspect legacy readiness on CLI-supported chains. Review beta or website-only legacy migration surfaces in the website.

Terminology
  Recovery phrase          24-word mnemonic by default in the CLI; imports may be 12/24 words.
  Signer key               Private key that pays gas and sends transactions.
  Pool Account             Your onchain deposit, tracked for withdrawal or ragequit recovery.
  0xBow ASP status  approved (withdraw ready), pending (waiting),
                                   poa_required (Proof of Association needed),
                                   declined (ragequit available), unknown (unresolved).
  Relayed withdrawal       Privacy-preserving withdrawal via a relayer (recommended).
  Direct withdrawal        Non-private withdrawal; links deposit and withdrawal onchain.
  Ragequit    Self-custody guarantee. Public, irreversible recovery to original deposit address.

Agent Integration
  For programmatic/agent use, run privacy-pools capabilities --agent to discover
  commands, schemas, supported chains, error codes, and the recommended workflow.
  Use privacy-pools describe <command...> --agent to inspect one command at runtime.

Further Reading
  privacy-pools <command> --help  (command-specific details and examples)
  Package-relative docs (open from a source checkout or installed package root):
  docs/reference.md   Flags, configuration, environment variables, project structure
  docs/runtime-upgrades.md  Native runtime troubleshooting, fallback controls, upgrade playbook
  AGENTS.md           Agent integration guide, JSON payloads, unsigned mode
  CHANGELOG.md        Release history and migration notes

  Run privacy-pools <command> --help for command-specific details.

  npm run dev -- status                        (from source, no global install)
  privacy-pools completion --help                  (shell autocomplete setup)

Quick Start
  privacy-pools init
  privacy-pools init --recovery-phrase-file <downloaded-file> (load an existing account from a website export)
  cat <downloaded-file> | privacy-pools init --recovery-phrase-stdin (stdin alternative for loading an account)
  privacy-pools init --signer-only (finish setup or replace the signer key without changing the account)
  privacy-pools flow start 0.1 ETH --to 0xRecipient          (easy path: deposit now, withdraw later)
  privacy-pools flow start 100 USDC --to 0xRecipient --new-wallet  (easy path with a dedicated workflow wallet)
  privacy-pools pools                                          (browse available pools)
  privacy-pools deposit 0.1 ETH
  privacy-pools accounts --chain mainnet --pending-only        (poll ASP review; keep the same --chain until it disappears)
  privacy-pools accounts --chain mainnet                       (then confirm approved vs declined vs POA Needed)
  privacy-pools withdraw 0.05 ETH --to 0xRecipient --pool-account PA-1
  Transaction commands use your default chain (set during init).
  Public dashboards like pools/activity/protocol-stats default to CLI-supported mainnet chains.
  Use --include-testnets to include supported testnets, or --chain to scope one network.
  Accounts is wallet-dependent: use --chain to keep approval checks on the same network as the deposit.

  Deposits are reviewed by the 0xBow ASP before approval.
  Most deposits are approved within 1 hour, but some may take longer (up to 7 days).
  ASP approval is required for withdraw, including --direct. Ragequit is your
  self-custody guarantee, always available to publicly recover funds to the
  original deposit address. Declined saved easy-path workflows use
  'flow ragequit' as their canonical public recovery path, and operators can
  also choose it manually after the public deposit exists.

Recovery Phrase and Signer Key
  Recovery phrase  restores this Privacy Pools account and enables private withdrawals
  Signer key       pays gas and sends transactions (can be set later with init --signer-only)
  The signer key may come from the same wallet as your recovery phrase or from a separate key you control.
  Note: PRIVACY_POOLS_PRIVATE_KEY env var takes precedence over a saved key file.
  Exception: flow start --new-wallet creates and uses a dedicated per-workflow wallet instead of the configured signer.

Workflow
  1. init           Set up your Privacy Pools account and config (run once)
  2. flow start     Easy path: deposit now and save a later private withdrawal
  3. flow watch     Human path: stay attached through funding, approval, delay, and withdrawal
  4. flow status    Agent path: poll the saved workflow snapshot without mutating it
  5. flow step      Agent path: advance at most one saved-workflow step
  6. flow ragequit  Self-custody recovery for saved workflows (always available)
  7. pools          Manual path: browse available pools
  8. deposit        Manual path: deposit into a pool (ASP vetting fee shown before confirming)
  9. accounts       Manual path: poll pending review, then confirm approval status and balances
 10. migrate status Read-only legacy check on CLI-supported chains
 11. withdraw       Manual path: withdraw privately (once approved; fee shown before confirming)
 12. history        View transaction history
  *  status         Check setup and connection health (checks run by default)
  *  upgrade        Check npm for updates or upgrade this CLI
  *  activity       Public onchain feed (for your history, use 'history')
  *  ragequit       Self-custody guarantee. Publicly recovers funds to deposit address
  *  withdraw quote Check relayer fees before withdrawing
  'migrate status' is read-only. The CLI does not submit migration transactions; use the website for actual migration or website-based recovery.
  It only checks chains currently supported by the CLI; review beta or website-only legacy migration surfaces in the website.
  Human 'flow start --new-wallet' waits for funding automatically; '--agent' returns an awaiting_funding snapshot instead.
  In machine mode, this path requires '--export-new-wallet <path>' so the generated key is backed up first.
  Manual commands remain available for advanced control.

Flow States
  awaiting_funding                    Workflow wallet needs ETH/tokens to proceed.
  depositing_publicly                 Deposit transaction submitted, awaiting onchain confirmation.
  awaiting_asp                        Deposit confirmed, ASP is reviewing.
  approved_waiting_privacy_delay      Approved; privacy delay timer running.
  approved_ready_to_withdraw          Ready for private withdrawal.
  withdrawing                         Withdrawal transaction in flight.
  completed                           Private withdrawal succeeded.
  completed_public_recovery           Ragequit recovery completed.
  paused_poa_required                 Proof of Association needed before private withdrawal.
  paused_declined                     Deposit declined; use flow ragequit for public recovery.
  stopped_external                    Workflow stopped by external event.

Ragequit / Public Recovery
  privacy-pools ragequit ETH --pool-account PA-1
  privacy-pools flow ragequit latest

  Ragequit publicly recovers a Pool Account to the original deposit address.
  It is your self-custody fallback when ASP approval is unavailable, a saved
  flow is declined, the relayer minimum blocks a saved full-balance withdrawal,
  or you explicitly choose public recovery instead of waiting.

  This does not provide privacy for that Pool Account. Prefer withdraw or
  flow watch when the account can continue through the private relayed path.
  In the website this corresponds to the Exit path.

Global Options
  -c, --chain <name>    Target chain (mainnet, arbitrum, optimism; testnets: sepolia, op-sepolia)
  -r, --rpc-url <url>   Override RPC URL
  -j, --json            Machine-readable JSON output
  --output <fmt>        Output format: table (default), csv, json, yaml, wide, name
  --no-color            Disable colored output (also respects NO_COLOR env var)
  -y, --yes             Skip confirmation prompts
  -q, --quiet           Suppress human-oriented stderr output
  -v, --verbose         Enable verbose/debug output (-v info, -vv debug, -vvv trace)
  --no-progress         Suppress spinners/progress indicators (useful in CI)
  --no-header          Suppress header rows in CSV and wide/tabular table output
  --agent               Alias for --json --yes --quiet (agent/automation mode)
  --timeout <seconds>  Network/transaction timeout (default: 30)
  --jmes <expr>        Filter JSON output with JMESPath syntax
  --jq <expr>          Compatibility alias for --jmes (not jq syntax)
  --template <template> Render structured output through a lightweight {{path.to.value}} template
  --web                 Open the primary explorer or portal link in your browser when available
  --no-banner           Disable welcome banner
  --profile <name>     Use a named profile (separate wallet identity and config)

Environment Variables
  PRIVACY_POOLS_HOME / PRIVACY_POOLS_CONFIG_DIR  Override the CLI config directory.
  XDG_CONFIG_HOME  Fallback config base. Used as $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/privacy-pools when no Privacy Pools override is set and no legacy ~/.privacy-pools directory exists.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_PRIVATE_KEY  Signer private key; takes precedence over the saved .signer file.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_CIRCUITS_DIR  Override the circuit artifact directory with a trusted pre-provisioned path.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RPC_URL / PP_RPC_URL  Override the RPC endpoint for all chains.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_ASP_HOST / PP_ASP_HOST  Override the ASP endpoint for all chains.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RELAYER_HOST / PP_RELAYER_HOST  Override the relayer endpoint for all chains.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RPC_URL_<CHAIN> / PP_RPC_URL_<CHAIN>  Override the RPC endpoint for one chain, for example ARBITRUM or SEPOLIA.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_ASP_HOST_<CHAIN> / PP_ASP_HOST_<CHAIN>  Override the ASP endpoint for one chain.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RELAYER_HOST_<CHAIN> / PP_RELAYER_HOST_<CHAIN>  Override the relayer endpoint for one chain.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_AGENT  Enable agent mode by default (equivalent to --agent).
  PRIVACY_POOLS_QUIET  Suppress human-oriented stderr output by default, matching --quiet.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_YES  Skip confirmation prompts by default, matching --yes.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_NO_PROGRESS  Suppress spinners/progress indicators by default, matching --no-progress.
  NO_COLOR  Disable colored output, matching --no-color.
  FORCE_COLOR  Force colored output when supported by the terminal renderer.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_BANNER / PRIVACY_POOLS_BANNER_ART  Choose the welcome illustration. Set to merkle for the alternate Merkle tree banner.
  PP_NO_UPDATE_CHECK  Set to 1 to disable the update-available notification.

Interaction Modes
  Human mode (default): interactive prompts + readable output.
  Agent mode: --agent for structured JSON output, no prompts, no banners.
  Equivalent to --json --yes --quiet.

Advanced Modes
  Unsigned mode builds transaction payloads without signing or submitting.
  Requires init (recovery phrase) for deposit secret generation.
  Does NOT require a signer key. The signing party provides their own.
  Output includes from plus description. from is null when the signer is unconstrained, and set when the protocol requires a specific caller.
  --unsigned           (default) Wrapped in JSON envelope: { schemaVersion, success, ... }
  --unsigned tx        Raw transaction array: [{ from, to, data, value, valueHex, chainId, description }]
             Raw format skips the envelope. Intended for direct piping to signing tools.
  --dry-run    Validate and generate proofs without submitting.

Environment Variable Fallbacks
  PRIVACY_POOLS_AGENT  Enable agent mode by default (equivalent to --agent).
  PRIVACY_POOLS_QUIET  Suppress human-oriented stderr output by default, matching --quiet.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_YES  Skip confirmation prompts by default, matching --yes.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_NO_PROGRESS  Suppress spinners/progress indicators by default, matching --no-progress.
  NO_COLOR  Disable colored output, matching --no-color.
  FORCE_COLOR  Force colored output when supported by the terminal renderer.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_BANNER / PRIVACY_POOLS_BANNER_ART  Choose the welcome illustration. Set to merkle for the alternate Merkle tree banner.
  PP_NO_UPDATE_CHECK  Set to 1 to disable the update-available notification.

TTY Behavior
  Interactive init, flow, deposit, withdraw, ragequit, upgrade, and recipient prompts require stdin/stdout TTYs.
  In non-TTY shells, provide the required flags or use --agent so the command fails closed with a machine-readable error instead of waiting for input.
  PP_FORCE_TTY=1 is test-only escape hatch behavior for preview runners and should not be used in automation.

Configuration Overrides
  PRIVACY_POOLS_HOME / PRIVACY_POOLS_CONFIG_DIR  Override the CLI config directory.
  XDG_CONFIG_HOME  Fallback config base. Used as $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/privacy-pools when no Privacy Pools override is set and no legacy ~/.privacy-pools directory exists.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_PRIVATE_KEY  Signer private key; takes precedence over the saved .signer file.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_CIRCUITS_DIR  Override the circuit artifact directory with a trusted pre-provisioned path.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RPC_URL / PP_RPC_URL  Override the RPC endpoint for all chains.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_ASP_HOST / PP_ASP_HOST  Override the ASP endpoint for all chains.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RELAYER_HOST / PP_RELAYER_HOST  Override the relayer endpoint for all chains.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RPC_URL_<CHAIN> / PP_RPC_URL_<CHAIN>  Override the RPC endpoint for one chain, for example ARBITRUM or SEPOLIA.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_ASP_HOST_<CHAIN> / PP_ASP_HOST_<CHAIN>  Override the ASP endpoint for one chain.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_RELAYER_HOST_<CHAIN> / PP_RELAYER_HOST_<CHAIN>  Override the relayer endpoint for one chain.

Runtime Overrides
  PRIVACY_POOLS_CLI_DISABLE_NATIVE  Set to 1 to force the pure JS runtime path.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_CLI_BINARY  Advanced maintainer override for the launcher target native-shell binary path.
  PRIVACY_POOLS_CLI_JS_WORKER  Advanced maintainer override for the packaged JS worker entrypoint.

nextActions
  Success JSON responses may include nextActions[] when the CLI has a low-ambiguity follow-up to recommend.
  Each entry includes command, reason, when, cliCommand, args?, options?, and runnable?.

Runnable Semantics
  runnable=true  The cliCommand is complete and can be executed as-is.
  runnable=false The action is a template; fill in missing user input first.

Ordering
  The first matching action is the highest-priority recommendation.
  Private/resume paths come first, required public recovery comes before optional public recovery, and template deposit/withdraw actions come last.

Common Uses
  privacy-pools deposit --agent         Emits the canonical pending-review polling command.
  privacy-pools status --agent          Emits the canonical init/pools/accounts follow-up.
  privacy-pools tx-status <submissionId> --agent  Polls async write confirmation after --no-wait.
  privacy-pools flow status --agent     Emits saved-workflow poll, step, or ragequit guidance.

Profiles
  Profiles let one machine keep separate Privacy Pools config, wallet state, and signer setup under different names.
  Use --profile <name> on any command to select one profile for that invocation.

Commands
  privacy-pools config profile list
  privacy-pools config profile create <name>
  privacy-pools config profile use <name>
  privacy-pools config profile active

When To Use Them
  Use profiles when you need separate operator identities, separate test/main workflows, or isolated machine-runner state on one host.

Pool Accounts
  A Pool Account (e.g. PA-1) is your onchain deposit.
  Approval, withdrawal, and ragequit happen per Pool Account, not per wallet.

Statuses
  approved       Ready for private withdrawal
  pending        Still under ASP review
  poa_required   Proof of Association is required before private withdrawal
  declined       Private withdrawal unavailable; ragequit remains available
  spent          Already withdrawn
  exited         Ragequit completed

Useful Commands
  privacy-pools accounts --chain <chain>
  privacy-pools withdraw --pool-account PA-1 --to 0xRecipient
  privacy-pools ragequit --pool-account PA-1
  privacy-pools migrate status --agent --include-testnets  (legacy readiness only)

Agent Mode
  --agent is shorthand for --json --yes --quiet.
  Structured JSON stays on stdout. Human-oriented narration stays on stderr and is suppressed in agent mode.

Discovery
  privacy-pools capabilities --agent      Full runtime capability manifest
  privacy-pools describe withdraw --agent Command-specific flags, risks, and JSON fields
  privacy-pools describe envelope.nextActions --agent Schema-path contract fields
  privacy-pools guide next-actions --agent Shared nextActions contract

Recommended Flow
  1. privacy-pools status --agent
  2. privacy-pools init --agent --default-chain <chain> --backup-file <path>
  3. privacy-pools pools --agent --chain <chain>
  4. privacy-pools flow start <amount> <asset> --to <address> --agent --chain <chain>
  5. privacy-pools flow status [workflowId|latest] --agent
  6. privacy-pools flow step [workflowId|latest] --agent
  7. privacy-pools tx-status <submissionId> --agent  (when using --no-wait on deposit/withdraw/ragequit/broadcast)

JSON Contract
  Use --json for machine-readable output. Use --agent for --json --yes --quiet.
  Successful commands emit:
  { "schemaVersion": "2.0.0", "success": true, ...payload }
  Failed commands emit:
  { "schemaVersion": "2.0.0", "success": false, "errorCode": "...", "error": { ... } }

Filtering
  --json <fields>       Select top-level fields by comma-separated name.
  --json=<fields>       Inline form for compact scripts and automation.
  --jmes <expression>     Apply a JMESPath expression to the final envelope.
  --jq <expression>       Compatibility alias for --jmes (not jq syntax).
  --template <template>   Interpolate the final envelope with {{path.to.value}} placeholders and {{#items}}...{{/items}} list iteration.
  Unknown selected fields fail with INPUT_UNKNOWN_JSON_FIELD, availableFields[], and did-you-mean suggestions when available.
  Invalid JMESPath expressions fail before command output is emitted.
  --json <fields>, --jmes/--jq, and --template are mutually exclusive.

Discovery
  privacy-pools capabilities --agent      Full command and schema manifest.
  privacy-pools describe withdraw --agent Command-specific fields and examples.
  privacy-pools describe envelope.commands.status.successFields --agent Deep contract schema fragments.

Output Formats
  --output table  Human-readable default.
  --output wide   Human-readable wide tables on stdout for tabular list commands.
  --output csv    CSV for supported read-only list/report commands.
  --json          Structured envelopes on stdout.

Command Names
  Collection commands use plural root names: accounts, pools, recipients.
  Detail views use positionals instead of singular aliases:
  privacy-pools pools ETH      Pool detail
  privacy-pools accounts --pool-account PA-1  Pool Account detail/filter
  For discoverability, pools list and pools ls are accepted aliases for pools.

Short Flag Conventions
  -c chain, -r RPC URL, -j JSON, -o output format.
  -y yes/confirm, -q quiet, -v verbose.
  -p is page on paginated public reports; use --pool-account for Pool Account selection.
  Prefer long flags in scripts when a flag controls funds or privacy posture.

Modes
  --yes       Skip confirmation prompts. Use only after the command inputs are fully reviewed.
  --dry-run   Preview validation and generated outputs without signing or submitting.
  --unsigned  Build transaction payloads without signing or submitting; implies --yes.
  --agent     Machine mode: --json --yes --quiet.

Safety Notes
  --dry-run and --unsigned may use approximate validation because no transaction is submitted.
  Fund-moving commands still surface warnings and privacy-cost manifests where relevant.
  Prefer relayed withdrawals. withdraw --direct is a stronger privacy hazard than ragequit.

Quiet Mode
  --quiet suppresses human-oriented stderr output while keeping machine-readable stdout intact.
  Use it in CI or scripts when you want less narration but do not want JSON mode.

Status Special Case
  privacy-pools status --quiet prints one concise stdout summary line instead of going fully silent.
  That keeps automation-friendly logs from looking like a hung process.

Related Modes
  --json keeps structured envelopes on stdout.
  --agent is shorthand for --json --yes --quiet.

Troubleshooting
  Stale data?      Commands auto-sync; force a full re-sync with 'privacy-pools sync'.
  ASP unreachable?  Check 'privacy-pools status' (health checks run by default).
  Long proof time?  Proofs use bundled circuit artifacts. The first proof may spend a moment verifying them.
  Native fallback?  Set PRIVACY_POOLS_CLI_DISABLE_NATIVE=1 to force the JS launcher,
                   or see docs/runtime-upgrades.md for runtime troubleshooting and overrides.
  Upgrade path?     Run privacy-pools upgrade to check npm for updates or upgrade this CLI.
  Not approved?     Deposits are reviewed by the ASP.
                   Most deposits are approved within 1 hour, but some may take longer (up to 7 days).
                   Some may require Proof of Association or be
                   declined. Declined deposits can be recovered
                   publicly via ragequit.
  Custom RPC?       Pass --rpc-url on any command, or save per-chain overrides in
                   ~/.privacy-pools/config.json under "rpcOverrides": { "<chainId>": "https://..." }.

Exit Codes
  0  Success
  1  Unknown/general error
  2  Input/validation error
  3  RPC/network error
  4  Setup required or incomplete local configuration
  5  Relayer error
  6  Proof generation error
  7  Contract revert
  8  ASP error
  9  Prompt or confirmation cancelled


Using CLI with an Existing Website Account
  If you already use privacypools.com, you can access the same account from the CLI:
  1. Export your 12/24-word recovery phrase from the website.
  2. Run: privacy-pools init --recovery-phrase-file ./recovery.txt (or: cat recovery.txt | privacy-pools init --recovery-phrase-stdin)
  3. Set your signer key: export PRIVACY_POOLS_PRIVATE_KEY=0x...
  4. Run: privacy-pools accounts  (syncs onchain state automatically)
  If the website account was created before the 2024 security update, run privacy-pools migrate status to inspect legacy readiness on CLI-supported chains. Review beta or website-only legacy migration surfaces in the website.

Terminology
  Recovery phrase          24-word mnemonic by default in the CLI; imports may be 12/24 words.
  Signer key               Private key that pays gas and sends transactions.
  Pool Account             Your onchain deposit, tracked for withdrawal or ragequit recovery.
  0xBow ASP status  approved (withdraw ready), pending (waiting),
                                   poa_required (Proof of Association needed),
                                   declined (ragequit available), unknown (unresolved).
  Relayed withdrawal       Privacy-preserving withdrawal via a relayer (recommended).
  Direct withdrawal        Non-private withdrawal; links deposit and withdrawal onchain.
  Ragequit    Self-custody guarantee. Public, irreversible recovery to original deposit address.

Agent Integration
  For programmatic/agent use, run privacy-pools capabilities --agent to discover
  commands, schemas, supported chains, error codes, and the recommended workflow.
  Use privacy-pools describe <command...> --agent to inspect one command at runtime.

Further Reading
  privacy-pools <command> --help  (command-specific details and examples)
  Package-relative docs (open from a source checkout or installed package root):
  docs/reference.md   Flags, configuration, environment variables, project structure
  docs/runtime-upgrades.md  Native runtime troubleshooting, fallback controls, upgrade playbook
  AGENTS.md           Agent integration guide, JSON payloads, unsigned mode
  CHANGELOG.md        Release history and migration notes

  Run privacy-pools <command> --help for command-specific details.

[1m[38;5;110mQuick Start:[39m[22m
  [38;5;110mprivacy-pools init[39m
  [38;5;110mprivacy-pools init --recovery-phrase-file <downloaded-file>[39m [38;5;248m(load an existing account from a website export)[39m
  [38;5;110mcat <downloaded-file> | privacy-pools init --recovery-phrase-stdin[39m [38;5;248m(stdin alternative for loading an account)[39m
  [38;5;110mprivacy-pools init --signer-only[39m [38;5;248m(finish setup or replace the signer key without changing the account)[39m
  [38;5;110mprivacy-pools flow start 0.1 ETH --to 0xRecipient[39m          [38;5;248m(easy path: deposit now, withdraw later)[39m
  [38;5;110mprivacy-pools flow start 100 USDC --to 0xRecipient --new-wallet[39m  [38;5;248m(easy path with a dedicated workflow wallet)[39m
  [38;5;110mprivacy-pools pools[39m                                          [38;5;248m(browse available pools)[39m
  [38;5;110mprivacy-pools deposit 0.1 ETH[39m
  [38;5;110mprivacy-pools accounts --chain mainnet --pending-only[39m        [38;5;248m(poll ASP review; keep the same --chain until it disappears)[39m
  [38;5;110mprivacy-pools accounts --chain mainnet[39m                       [38;5;248m(then confirm approved vs declined vs POA Needed)[39m
  [38;5;110mprivacy-pools withdraw 0.05 ETH --to 0xRecipient --pool-account PA-1[39m
[38;5;248m  Transaction commands use your default chain (set during init).[39m
[38;5;248m  Public dashboards like pools/activity/protocol-stats default to CLI-supported mainnet chains.[39m
[38;5;248m  Use --include-testnets to include supported testnets, or --chain to scope one network.[39m
[38;5;248m  Accounts is wallet-dependent: use --chain to keep approval checks on the same network as the deposit.[39m

[38;5;248m  Deposits are reviewed by the 0xBow ASP before approval.[39m
[38;5;248m  Most deposits are approved within 1 hour, but some may take longer (up to 7 days).[39m
[38;5;248m  ASP approval is required for withdraw, including --direct. Ragequit is your[39m
[38;5;248m  self-custody guarantee, always available to publicly recover funds to the[39m
[38;5;248m  original deposit address. Declined saved easy-path workflows use[39m
[38;5;248m  'flow ragequit' as their canonical public recovery path, and operators can[39m
[38;5;248m  also choose it manually after the public deposit exists.[39m

[38;5;145mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[39m
[1m[38;5;110mRecovery Phrase and Signer Key:[39m[22m
  [38;5;179mRecovery phrase[39m  restores this Privacy Pools account and enables private withdrawals
  [38;5;179mSigner key[39m       pays gas and sends transactions (can be set later with init --signer-only)
  The signer key may come from the same wallet as your recovery phrase or from a separate key you control.
  Note: [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_PRIVATE_KEY[39m env var takes precedence over a saved key file.
  Exception: [38;5;110mflow start --new-wallet[39m creates and uses a dedicated per-workflow wallet instead of the configured signer.

[38;5;145mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[39m
[1m[38;5;110mWorkflow:[39m[22m
  1. [38;5;110minit[39m           Set up your Privacy Pools account and config (run once)
  2. [38;5;110mflow start[39m     Easy path: deposit now and save a later private withdrawal
  3. [38;5;110mflow watch[39m     Human path: stay attached through funding, approval, delay, and withdrawal
  4. [38;5;110mflow status[39m    Agent path: poll the saved workflow snapshot without mutating it
  5. [38;5;110mflow step[39m      Agent path: advance at most one saved-workflow step
  6. [38;5;110mflow ragequit[39m  Self-custody recovery for saved workflows (always available)
  7. [38;5;110mpools[39m          Manual path: browse available pools
  8. [38;5;110mdeposit[39m        Manual path: deposit into a pool (ASP vetting fee shown before confirming)
  9. [38;5;110maccounts[39m       Manual path: poll pending review, then confirm approval status and balances
 10. [38;5;110mmigrate status[39m Read-only legacy check on CLI-supported chains
 11. [38;5;110mwithdraw[39m       Manual path: withdraw privately (once approved; fee shown before confirming)
 12. [38;5;110mhistory[39m        View transaction history
  *  [38;5;110mstatus[39m         Check setup and connection health (checks run by default)
  *  [38;5;110mupgrade[39m        Check npm for updates or upgrade this CLI
  *  [38;5;110mactivity[39m       Public onchain feed [38;5;248m(for your history, use 'history')[39m
  *  [38;5;110mragequit[39m       Self-custody guarantee. Publicly recovers funds to deposit address
  *  [38;5;110mwithdraw quote[39m Check relayer fees before withdrawing
[38;5;248m  'migrate status' is read-only. The CLI does not submit migration transactions; use the website for actual migration or website-based recovery.[39m
[38;5;248m  It only checks chains currently supported by the CLI; review beta or website-only legacy migration surfaces in the website.[39m
[38;5;248m  Human 'flow start --new-wallet' waits for funding automatically; '--agent' returns an awaiting_funding snapshot instead.[39m
[38;5;248m  In machine mode, this path requires '--export-new-wallet <path>' so the generated key is backed up first.[39m
[38;5;248m  Manual commands remain available for advanced control.[39m

[38;5;145mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[39m
[1m[38;5;110mFlow States:[39m[22m
  [38;5;179mawaiting_funding[39m                    Workflow wallet needs ETH/tokens to proceed.
  [38;5;179mdepositing_publicly[39m                 Deposit transaction submitted, awaiting onchain confirmation.
  [38;5;179mawaiting_asp[39m                        Deposit confirmed, ASP is reviewing.
  [38;5;179mapproved_waiting_privacy_delay[39m      Approved; privacy delay timer running.
  [38;5;179mapproved_ready_to_withdraw[39m          Ready for private withdrawal.
  [38;5;179mwithdrawing[39m                         Withdrawal transaction in flight.
  [38;5;179mcompleted[39m                           Private withdrawal succeeded.
  [38;5;179mcompleted_public_recovery[39m           Ragequit recovery completed.
  [38;5;179mpaused_poa_required[39m                 Proof of Association needed before private withdrawal.
  [38;5;179mpaused_declined[39m                     Deposit declined; use flow ragequit for public recovery.
  [38;5;179mstopped_external[39m                    Workflow stopped by external event.

[38;5;145mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[39m
[1m[38;5;110mRagequit / Public Recovery:[39m[22m
  [38;5;110mprivacy-pools ragequit ETH --pool-account PA-1[39m
  [38;5;110mprivacy-pools flow ragequit latest[39m

[38;5;248m  Ragequit publicly recovers a Pool Account to the original deposit address.[39m
[38;5;248m  It is your self-custody fallback when ASP approval is unavailable, a saved[39m
[38;5;248m  flow is declined, the relayer minimum blocks a saved full-balance withdrawal,[39m
[38;5;248m  or you explicitly choose public recovery instead of waiting.[39m

[38;5;248m  This does not provide privacy for that Pool Account. Prefer withdraw or[39m
[38;5;248m  flow watch when the account can continue through the private relayed path.[39m
[38;5;248m  In the website this corresponds to the Exit path.[39m

[38;5;145mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[39m
[1m[38;5;110mGlobal Options:[39m[22m
  [38;5;179m-c, --chain <name>[39m    Target chain (mainnet, arbitrum, optimism; testnets: sepolia, op-sepolia)
  [38;5;179m-r, --rpc-url <url>[39m   Override RPC URL
  [38;5;179m-j, --json[39m            Machine-readable JSON output
  [38;5;179m--output <fmt>[39m        Output format: table (default), csv, json, yaml, wide, name
  [38;5;179m--no-color[39m            Disable colored output (also respects NO_COLOR env var)
  [38;5;179m-y, --yes[39m             Skip confirmation prompts
  [38;5;179m-q, --quiet[39m           Suppress human-oriented stderr output
  [38;5;179m-v, --verbose[39m         Enable verbose/debug output (-v info, -vv debug, -vvv trace)
  [38;5;179m--no-progress[39m         Suppress spinners/progress indicators (useful in CI)
  [38;5;179m--no-header[39m          Suppress header rows in CSV and wide/tabular table output
  [38;5;179m--agent[39m               Alias for --json --yes --quiet (agent/automation mode)
  [38;5;179m--timeout <seconds>[39m  Network/transaction timeout (default: 30)
  [38;5;179m--jmes <expr>[39m        Filter JSON output with JMESPath syntax
  [38;5;179m--jq <expr>[39m          Compatibility alias for --jmes (not jq syntax)
  [38;5;179m--template <template>[39m Render structured output through a lightweight {{path.to.value}} template
  [38;5;179m--web[39m                 Open the primary explorer or portal link in your browser when available
  [38;5;179m--no-banner[39m           Disable welcome banner
  [38;5;179m--profile <name>[39m     Use a named profile (separate wallet identity and config)

[38;5;145mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[39m
[1m[38;5;110mEnvironment Variables:[39m[22m
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_HOME / PRIVACY_POOLS_CONFIG_DIR[39m  Override the CLI config directory.
  [38;5;179mXDG_CONFIG_HOME[39m  Fallback config base. Used as $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/privacy-pools when no Privacy Pools override is set and no legacy ~/.privacy-pools directory exists.
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_PRIVATE_KEY[39m  Signer private key; takes precedence over the saved .signer file.
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_CIRCUITS_DIR[39m  Override the circuit artifact directory with a trusted pre-provisioned path.
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_RPC_URL / PP_RPC_URL[39m  Override the RPC endpoint for all chains.
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_ASP_HOST / PP_ASP_HOST[39m  Override the ASP endpoint for all chains.
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_RELAYER_HOST / PP_RELAYER_HOST[39m  Override the relayer endpoint for all chains.
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_RPC_URL_<CHAIN> / PP_RPC_URL_<CHAIN>[39m  Override the RPC endpoint for one chain, for example ARBITRUM or SEPOLIA.
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_ASP_HOST_<CHAIN> / PP_ASP_HOST_<CHAIN>[39m  Override the ASP endpoint for one chain.
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_RELAYER_HOST_<CHAIN> / PP_RELAYER_HOST_<CHAIN>[39m  Override the relayer endpoint for one chain.
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_AGENT[39m  Enable agent mode by default (equivalent to --agent).
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_QUIET[39m  Suppress human-oriented stderr output by default, matching --quiet.
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_YES[39m  Skip confirmation prompts by default, matching --yes.
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_NO_PROGRESS[39m  Suppress spinners/progress indicators by default, matching --no-progress.
  [38;5;179mNO_COLOR[39m  Disable colored output, matching --no-color.
  [38;5;179mFORCE_COLOR[39m  Force colored output when supported by the terminal renderer.
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_BANNER / PRIVACY_POOLS_BANNER_ART[39m  Choose the welcome illustration. Set to merkle for the alternate Merkle tree banner.
  [38;5;179mPP_NO_UPDATE_CHECK[39m  Set to 1 to disable the update-available notification.

[38;5;145mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[39m
[1m[38;5;110mInteraction Modes:[39m[22m
  Human mode (default): interactive prompts + readable output.
  Agent mode: --agent for structured JSON output, no prompts, no banners.
  Equivalent to --json --yes --quiet.

[38;5;145mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[39m
[1m[38;5;110mAdvanced Modes:[39m[22m
  Unsigned mode builds transaction payloads without signing or submitting.
  Requires init (recovery phrase) for deposit secret generation.
  Does NOT require a signer key. The signing party provides their own.
  Output includes [38;5;248mfrom[39m plus [38;5;248mdescription[39m. [38;5;248mfrom[39m is null when the signer is unconstrained, and set when the protocol requires a specific caller.
  [38;5;179m--unsigned[39m           (default) Wrapped in JSON envelope: { schemaVersion, success, ... }
  [38;5;179m--unsigned tx[39m        Raw transaction array: [{ from, to, data, value, valueHex, chainId, description }]
             Raw format skips the envelope. Intended for direct piping to signing tools.
  [38;5;179m--dry-run[39m    Validate and generate proofs without submitting.

[38;5;145mв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ[39m
[1m[38;5;110mEnvironment Variable Fallbacks:[39m[22m
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_AGENT[39m  Enable agent mode by default (equivalent to --agent).
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_QUIET[39m  Suppress human-oriented stderr output by default, matching --quiet.
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_YES[39m  Skip confirmation prompts by default, matching --yes.
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_NO_PROGRESS[39m  Suppress spinners/progress indicators by default, matching --no-progress.
  [38;5;179mNO_COLOR[39m  Disable colored output, matching --no-color.

[1m[38;5;110mTTY Behavior:[39m[22m
  Interactive init, flow, deposit, withdraw, ragequit, upgrade, and recipient prompts require stdin/stdout TTYs.
  In non-TTY shells, provide the required flags or use --agent so the command fails closed with a machine-readable error instead of waiting for input.
  [38;5;179mPP_FORCE_TTY=1[39m is test-only escape hatch behavior for preview runners and should not be used in automation.

[1m[38;5;110mConfiguration Overrides:[39m[22m
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_HOME / PRIVACY_POOLS_CONFIG_DIR[39m  Override the CLI config directory.

  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_PRIVATE_KEY[39m  Signer private key; takes precedence over the saved .signer file.

  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_RPC_URL / PP_RPC_URL[39m  Override the RPC endpoint for all chains.
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_ASP_HOST / PP_ASP_HOST[39m  Override the ASP endpoint for all chains.
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_RELAYER_HOST / PP_RELAYER_HOST[39m  Override the relayer endpoint for all chains.
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_RPC_URL_<CHAIN> / PP_RPC_URL_<CHAIN>[39m  Override the RPC endpoint for one chain, for example ARBITRUM or SEPOLIA.
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_ASP_HOST_<CHAIN> / PP_ASP_HOST_<CHAIN>[39m  Override the ASP endpoint for one chain.
  [38;5;179mPRIVACY_POOLS_RELAYER_HOST_<CHAIN> / PP_RELAYER_HOST_<CHAIN>[39m  Override the relayer endpoint for one chain.

  [38;5;179m--json <fields>[39m       Select top-level fields by comma-separated name.

  [38;5;179m--jmes <expression>[39m     Apply a JMESPath expression to the final envelope.
  [38;5;179m--jq <expression>[39m       Compatibility alias for --jmes (not jq syntax).

  --json <fields>, --jmes/--jq, and --template are mutually exclusive.

  [38;5;179m-c[39m chain, [38;5;179m-r[39m RPC URL, [38;5;179m-j[39m JSON, [38;5;179m-o[39m output format.

  --dry-run and --unsigned may use approximate validation because no transaction is submitted.
